calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Summary Judgment And Vacate Forfeiture And Exonerate Bail Bond Pursuant To Penal Code Section 1305(B)
Off Calendar Jul-24-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance.

RULING: The Court adopted the tentative ruling which was uncontested. DEFENDANT SEAVIEW INSURANCE CO.'S Motion To Set Aside Summary Judgment And Vacate Forfeiture And Exonerate Bail Bond Pursuant To Penal Code Section 1305(B) is granted. Penal Code sec. 1305(b) explicitly provides that obligations under the bond are released if the clerk fails to mail a copy of the notice of forfeiture to the bail agent at the address shown on the bond. The address for the bail agent shown on the bond is a San Jose address. (See Ex. A attached to the Motion.) The address where the clerk sent the notice of forfeiture is a Carlsbad address. (See Ex. C attached to the Motion.) "Penal Code sections 1305 and 1306 are subject to precise and strict construction." (People v. Nat. Automobile and Casualty Ins. Co. (2002) 98 Cal.App.4th 277, 287-88.) Plaintiff has not established that Defendant had timely actual notice of the forfeiture. The Court SIGNED the ORDER that was submitted by email. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR# 5976.
